page: Microsoft Azure E4bds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EBDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512F, AVX512_VNNI, AES, and SHA_ni. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It also includes 154 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, designed for enhanced block storage performance. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data reveals a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by bottom-25% results in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average at 25.57 GB/sec, whereas read bandwidth is weak, measuring 29.48 GB/sec for large blocks. Cryptographic capabilities are solid, with the PassMark CPU encryption test achieving average tier results.

Given the 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage capacity, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dense parallel compute. The weak multi-core scaling and lower database operations benchmark suggest it is less suited for highly concurrent transactional databases or heavy compilation tasks. Instead, it fits workloads such as moderate-scale in-memory caching, single-threaded data processing, static web serving, and lightweight text processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E4bds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EBDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. The hardware configuration delivers a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for memory-intensive operations. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, though multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles. The server lacks GPU acceleration. This resource profile is best suited for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power, such as moderate in-memory caching, static web serving, and single-threaded data processing tasks, rather than highly parallelized compute or heavy database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4bds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-intensive, moderate-compute workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Suited for in-memory caching, static web serving, and light text processing
